title: Multiplexed Fixed Single Cell RNA Sequencing of  frozen human post mortem brain
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f3r1","text":"This protocol describes the processing of post-mortem frozen brain tissue for fixed single-cell sequencing using 10x Genomics for the library preparation. This protocol can be applied and generalized for infectious tissues that need to be fixed before processing. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"c1h1n","text":"For this protocol, frozen brain tissue from liquid nitrogen is recommended. The samples may be highly variable in cell counts and quality due to various dependencies, such as disease status, the time it took to transfer the samples into liquid nitrogen after dissection, the time elapsed between the patient's death and the dissection, and the brain region being observed. This protocol was performed using striatal tissue.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b820s","text":"Using ~30mg of tissue led to 100.000-400.000 cells counted in FACS, which resulted in 2000-13,000 unique cells sequenced.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qaq4","text":"To get an estimate on the quality of the sample, a percentage was sorted to single cell fraction with FACS.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"558sj","text":"5-20% or greater single cells is optimal, and it is safe to continue. A higher yield of 6,000-13,000 cells in the end may be expected.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a77vt","text":"2-5% Single Cells are of average quality to continue, if the result of 3,000-6,000 Cells are enough for analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7g1qt","text":"Less than 2% indicates a poor cell quality, and a low yield of reads with poor quality is expected even if a high number of Cells (e.g. 200.000) have been collected in the Single Cell Fraction. Continue at own risk.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":99,"length":5},{"style":"bold","offset":203,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"98s56","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

MiSeq, NextSeq 500/550, NextSeq 1000/2000, NovaSeq","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Note: Nuclei Isolation is following the standard 10x Genomics "Nuclei Isolation" and "Fixation Protocol" with some tweaks for maximizing Nuclei yield: https://www.10xgenomics.com/support/flex-gene-expression/documentation/steps/sample-prep/chromium-nuclei-isolation-kit-sample-prep-user-guide https://www.10xgenomics.com/support/flex-gene-expression/documentation/steps/sample-prep/fixation-of-cells-and-nuclei-for-chromium-single-cell-gene-expression-flex Preparation of reagents and centrifuge

Cell sorting is mandatory, as ~95% of the pellet will be debris or clumps If the cells have been fixed overnight, continue with the cell sorting. Note: Ensure that all samples have been fixed for the same duration.
Nuclei Isolation
Pre-chill the centrifuge to 4 °C .
Obtain materials and reagents as listed:
Cut 30 mg of the frozen tissue on dry ice and put it in the sample dissociation tube.
Add 200µl of Lysis Buffer to the tissue On ice .
Homogenize the tissue until no clumps remain, then add an additional 300 µL of Lysis Buffer and mix 10 times with a pipette.
Incubate for 00:10:00 On ice .
Transfer the lysate to the Nuclei Isolation Column.
Centrifuge 16.000 rcf, °C, for 00:00:20 .
Keep the Collection Tube and discard the column. Resuspend the nuclei by vortexing the Collection Tube for 00:00:10 at 3.200 rpm, °C, .
Centrifuge the Collection Tube 00:03:00 at 500 rcf, °C, .
Discard the supernatant. Strongly recommended: a small amount of 200 µL may be left behind if a low yield is expected.
Add 500 µL Debris Removal Reagent and resuspend by pipetting.
Centrifuge at 700 rcf, °C, for 00:10:00 .

Discard the supernatant. Note: The nuclei will be sitting at the bottom-side of the tube, and there will be a lot of debris/fat on the upper side of the tube. Gently take off the supernatant from the side of the wall, without going too low to take up the nuclei. Strongly recommended: an amount of 200 µL may be left behind if a low yield is expected.
Resuspend in 1 mL Wash and Resuspension Buffer.
Centrifuge at 500 rcf, °C, for 00:05:00 .
Discard the supernatant. Strongly recommended: a small amount of 100 µL may be left behind if a low yield is expected.
Add 1 mL of Fixation Buffer, resuspend by pipetting and leave at 4 °C for 16:00:00 - 24:00:00 .
Pooling and Washing of the samples
Prepare the following buffer: Use the upper table, if multiplexing 4 samples, and the lower table for up to 16 samples
Prepare the following reagents and materials as listed:
Prepare the buffers and reagents and place On ice : -Quenching Buffer: -PBS+2% BSA: 4 mL PBS 1 mL 10% BSA -300µM DAPI: Dilute 300 µL 1mM DAPI stock solution with 700 µL of PBS.

Pool the samples, using the cell count you got after hybrdization in a 15ml Falcon. NOTE: Usually, take all nuclei of the sample with the lowest concentration, and an equal amount of nuclei from the other samples to create one pool, where there are similar amount of cells from each sample. If one sample has way lower concentration than the others, it also worked out to Pool the full amount of all samples. But expect less reads from the sample with the low concentration.
Prepare GEM Master Mix + Sample Dilution
Prepare Master Mix On ice . Pipette mix 15x and centrifuge briefly.
Prepare the following reagents and materials as listed:
Post-Gem Incubation-Recovery
Add 125 µL Recovery Agent to each sample at Room temperature . DO NOT pipette mix or vortex the biphasic mixture.
Mix by inverting 5x and incubate for 00:02:00 .
Centrifuge briefly and slowly remove 125 µL Recovery Agent (pink phase). Note: A small amount of 20 µL Recovery Agent is normal and does not affect the performance.
Pre-Amplification PCR
Prepare Pre-Amplification Mix on ice. Vortex and centrifuge briefly.
Add 35 µL Pre-Amplification Mix to the aqueous sample.
Mix by inverting 8x and centrifuge briefly.
Incubate in a Thermocycler using the following protocol:
Store at 4 °C for up to 72:00:00 or -20 °C for 1 week, or proceed to the nextstep.
DNA Cleanup- SPRISelect
Prepare the Elution Solution. Vortex briefly.

Centrifuge the sample for 00:00:30 in a microcentrifuge and transfer 70 µL of the upper layer to a fresh 8-tube strip. Note: If a low yield is expected, transfer all of the upper layer and adjust the Volume of SPRIselect accordingly.
Vortex to resuspend the SPRIselect reagent. Add 126 µL SPRIselect reagent (1.8X) to each sample and pipette mix 15x. Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Place on the magnet "high" until the solution clears.
Remove the supernatant and wash the beads with 200 µL 80% Ethanol. Wait for 00:00:30 .
Remove the ethanol and repeat the previous step for a total of 2 washes.
Centrifuge briefly and place on the magnet "low" . Remove any remaining ethanol, but do not let the beads dry out.
Remove from the magnet. Add 101 µL Elution Solution. Wait 00:01:00 beforeresuspending. Pipette mix 15x.
Incubate 00:02:00 at Room temperature .
Place the tube strip on the magnet "High" until the solution clears.
Transfer 100 µL supernatant to a new 8-tube strip.
Store at 4 °C for up to 72:00:00 or -20 °C for 1 week, or proceed to the nextstep.
Prepare the following reagents and materials as listed:
Choose an appropriate index so there are no overlaps during one sequencing run.
Prepare the Sample Index PCR Mix On ice :
Transfer only 20 µL of the cleaned up sample to a new strip and add 60 µL of the prepared Sample Index PCR Mix.
Add 20 µL of an individual Dual Index TS Set A to each sample. Pipette mix 5x.

Incubate in a Thermal Cycler using the following protocol: Depending on the targeted cell recovery, choose the number of cycles from the Table below. Note: For analyzing 10.000 cells/barcode (40.000 in total), choose 10 cycles for the PCR program.
Store at 4 °C for ≤ 72:00:00 , or proceed to the next step.
Prepare the Wash and Resuspension Buffer and place On ice .
Prepare Debris Removal Buffer and place On ice .
Prepare Lysis Buffer and place On ice .
Obtain the 10x Nuclei Isolation Kit, Nuclease-Free Water, PBS, and 10x BSA.
FACS Sorting
Centrifuge fixed cells at 900 rcf, °C, 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Take off the supernatant. Note: the pellet should be more compact now, and the full removal of supernatant is possible.
Resuspend the pellet in 500 µL cold Quenching Buffer and incubate 00:05:00 On ice .
Centrifuge cells at 900 rcf, °C, 00:05:00 at Room temperature .

Remove supernatant and resuspend in 200 µL - 500 µL PBS+2% BSA. Note: if the cell suspension is too dense for FACS sorting, adjust with PBS+2%BSA to achieve a sort rate of ~10000 events/second during sorting to avoid clotting of the machine.
Add 5 µL 300µM DAPI to 1000 µL of cell suspension.
Pass the sample through a 30µm strainer directly into the FACS sorting tubes.

Sort the single DAPI positive events to a fresh 1.5 ml LoBind Tube and place On ice . Note: Each sample will take about 30 minutes to sort Depending on tissue quality, expect 50000-300000 single nuclei If it is less than 100000 nuclei, it is not recommended to continue as there is more nuclei loss expected during the preparation of the samples

Note: Library preparation is following the standard 10x Genomics "Chromium Fixed RNA Profiling Reagent Kits for Multiplexed Samples" protocol with some tweaks for maximizing nuclei yield: https://www.10xgenomics.com/support/flex-gene-expression/documentation/steps/library-prep/chromium-single-cell-gene-expression-flex-reagent-kits-for-multiplexed-samples Prepare the following reagents as listed:
Prepare the Hyb Mix at Room temperature :
Incubate Hyb Mix at 42 °C for 00:05:00 .

Centrifuge fixed nuclei at 900 x g, °C, for 00:05:00 and take off the Supernatant. Very Important: When removing the supernatant, leave about 20 µL behind. This drastically increases the yield and does not affect the hybridization performance.
Incubate samples for 16-24h at 42 °C in a thermocycler with heated lid. Continue with counting and GEM generation the next day.
Add 20 µL of Unique Human WTA-Probe Barcode per sample and gently mix by pipetting.
Resuspend the pellet in 80 µL Hyb Mix and transfer to a 8-strip tube. Keep the samples at Room temperature
Centrifuge at 900 x g, °C, for 00:05:00 and take off the supernatant.
Incubate at 42 °C for 00:10:00 .
Resuspend cell pellet in 1 mL Post-Hyb Wash Buffer and transfer to a 1.5ml Tube.
Centrifuge the pool at 900 x g, °C, for 00:05:00 and take off the supernatant.
Add 3 mL Post-Hyb Wash Buffer and mix by inverting 5 times.
Critical step : Filter the nuclei through a 30µm Strainer into a new 1.5ml Tube, to avoid clumps going into the GEM generation.
Incubate at 42 °C for 00:10:00 .
Resuspend cell pellet in 0.5 mL Post-Hyb Wash Buffer.
Resuspend the pellet in an appropriate amount of Post-Hyb Resuspension Buffer and place On ice :
Prepare Post-Hyb Resuspension Buffer and maintain at 4 °C :
Centrifuge at 900 x g, °C, for 00:05:00 and take off the supernatant.

Count the nuclei and calculate the total amount. Note: The lowest concentration should be 2000 nuclei/µl so 1.000.000 in total. If there are less nuclei, centrifuge at 900 x g, °C, for 00:05:00 and remove as much supernatant as needed, to achieve a concentration of 2000 nuclei/µl. For example if you have 100.000 Nuclei in 500µl, remove 450µl of supernatant after centrifugation to achieve a concentration of 2000 Nuclei/µl in 50µl.
Add 35 µL of prepared GEM Master Mix into each tube containing the cell dilution and immediately proceed to the next step. Note: Don´t mix the sample at this point.

In a PCR 8-tube strip, prepare a dilution depending on the cell stock concentration and target cell recovery. Note: If you have a concentration of 2000 Cells/µl and aim for a Cell Recovery of 40000 cells, dilute 33 µL of Cell Stock with 7 µL Post-Hyb Resupension Buffer.
Assembly and Loading of the Next GEM Chip Q

Attach the gasket, with the smooth side facing the chip holder Open the chip holder Remove the chip from the bag, align the chip with the notch on the left side of the Holder and secure the chip with the clip on the right side The chip is now ready for loading Do not touch the smooth side of the gasket
When loading the chip, ensure the chip lays flat and raise the pipette tip as you dispense. This should take 00:00:05 .
Add 50% glycerol solution to each unused well: 70 μl in each unused well in row labeled 1 50 μl in each unused well in row labeled 2 150 μl in each unused well in row labeled 3

Prepare Gel Beads: Snap the tube strip holder with the Gel Bead strip into a10x Vortex Adapter. Vortex 00:00:30 . Centrifuge the Gel Bead strip for 00:00:05 .Confirm there are no bubbles at the bottom of the tubes & theliquid levels are even. Place the Gel Bead strip back in the holder. Secure the holder lid.

Load Row 1: With pipette set to 70 μl, gently pipette mix the GEM Master Mix+ Sample 15x. Using the same pipette tips, dispense 70 µL GEM Master Mix +Sample into the bottom center of wells in row labeled 1 withoutintroducing bubbles.
Load Row Labeled 2: Puncture the foil seal of the Gel Bead tubes. Slowly aspirate 50 µL Gel Beads. Dispense into the wells in row labeled 2 without introducingbubbles. Wait 00:01:00 .
Prepare for Run: Close the lid (gasket already attached). DO NOT touch thesmooth side of the gasket. DO NOT press down on the top of thegasket.
Load Row Labeled 3: Dispense 45 µL Partitioning Oil into the wells in row labeled 3from a reagent reservoir.
Run the Chromium X/iX

Press the eject button on the Chromium X to eject the tray.If the eject button is not touched within 1 min, tray will closeautomatically. System requires a few seconds before the tray can beejected again. Place the assembled chip with the gasket in the tray, ensuring that thechip stays horizontal. Press the button to retract the tray. Press the play button. At completion of the run ( 00:05:30 ), Chromium X/iX will chime. Immediately proceed to the next step.
Transfer GEMs

Place a tube strip on ice. Press the eject button of the Chromium X/iX and remove the chip. Discard the gasket. Open the chip holder. Fold the lid back until it clicksto expose the wells at 45 degrees. Check the volume in rows labeled 1-2. Abnormally high volume in anywell indicates a clog. Slowly aspirate 100 µL GEMs from the lowest points of the recovery wells in the top row labeled 3, without creating a seal between the tips and the bottom of the wells. Withdraw pipette tips from the wells. GEMs should appear opaque anduniform across all channels. Over the course of 00:00:20 , dispense GEMs into the tube strip on ice withthe pipette tips against the sidewalls of the tubes.
GEM Incubation
Incubate in a Thermal Cycler using the following protocol: Note: Store at 4 °C for up to a week, or proceed immediately to the next step.

Count the nuclei using 5 µL . Note: A high nuclei loss can occur here. For example, starting with 150,000-400,000 nuclei after FACS can result in 35,000-140,000 after probe hybridization and then a subsequent final read count of 3,500-13,000 per sample.
Post Sample Index PCR Size Selection – SPRIselect
Vortex to resuspend the SPRIselect reagent. Add 100 µL SPRIselect Reagent (1.0X) to each sample. Pipette mix 15x and incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Place on the magnet "high" until the solution clears.
Remove the supernatant and wash the beads with 200 µL 80% ethanol. Wait for 00:00:30 .
Remove the ethanol and repeat the previous step for a total of 2 washes.
Place the tube strip on the magnet "low" until the solution clears.
Remove from the magnet. Add 41 µL Buffer EB. Pipette mix 15x.
Centrifuge briefly and place on the magnet "low" . Remove any remaining Ethanol, but do not let the beads dry out.
Store at 4 °C for up to 72:00:00 or at -20 °C for long-term storage.
Transfer 40 µL to a new tube strip. The Library is now finished and can be prepared for Illumina Sequencing as needed.
Post Library Construction QC

Run 1 µL sample at 1:80 dilution on an Agilent Bioanalyzer High Sensitivity chip or on a Tapestation D5000 Screen Tape Assays. Select the region between 150-300 bp to determine average size of thelibrary.
Example of a good sort with a high fraction of 13.6% single DAPI positive events.
creator: Christoph Much
